skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X actions through the inference.sh CLI, including posts, media posts, likes, retweets, direct messages, follows, profile lookups, and post deletion.
skill.summary: Run Twitter/X publishing and engagement workflows with inference.sh CLI apps.
skill.author: inference-sh

security_audit.summary: Static findings were partly confirmed. The Markdown backtick and weak cryptography alerts are false positives, but the skill includes a real pipe-to-shell installer and many external commands that can modify a Twitter/X account through inference.sh. No evidence found of prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent, so this is high risk rather than blocked.

high_findings.title: Remote Install Script Piped to Shell
high_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The quick start tells users to run curl against https://cli.inference.sh and pipe the response directly to sh. This executes remote code without showing an integrity check, version pin, or review step before login.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell installer appears on line 22. This is a well-known high-risk installation pattern because the executed content is fetched at runtime.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands Trigger Social Account Actions
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill provides many infsh app run examples that can post, delete, like, retweet, send direct messages, follow users, and generate media through third-party services. These are intended capabilities, but they create real external side effects and require careful user consent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and use the allowed infsh CLI to perform live account operations. The behavior is legitimate for the skill purpose, but the side effects are clear.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Service URLs
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE with low severity: The skill references inference.sh installation and documentation URLs, plus a placeholder media URL. These are expected for this integration, but users must trust the external service and hosted media source.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are directly visible in the Markdown. They point to the advertised provider and documentation, so the risk is trust and supply-chain exposure rather than covert exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Execution Alerts
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer labeled Markdown code fences and inline code spans as Ruby or shell backtick execution. SKILL.md is documentation, not Ruby source; executable command behavior is evaluated separately in the external CLI findings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported locations are Markdown tables, code fences, or inline code spans in SKILL.md. No Ruby runtime or command-substitution syntax is present in an executable source file.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts point to the YAML description line and a Markdown table header. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function call, or crypto configuration appears at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 starts the description metadata and line 30 is a table header. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak crypto use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Install Script Piped to Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The quick start tells users to run curl against https://cli.inference.sh and pipe the response directly to sh. This executes remote code without showing an integrity check, version pin, or review step before login.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell installer appears on line 22. This is a well-known high-risk installation pattern because the executed content is fetched at runtime.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Commands Trigger Social Account Actions
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill provides many infsh app run examples that can post, delete, like, retweet, send direct messages, follow users, and generate media through third-party services. These are intended capabilities, but they create real external side effects and require careful user consent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and use the allowed infsh CLI to perform live account operations. The behavior is legitimate for the skill purpose, but the side effects are clear.

content.value_statement: Managing Twitter/X actions manually is slow and inconsistent across campaigns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured prompts for running inference.sh CLI workflows for posting, engagement, messaging, and account lookups.
actual_capabilities: Post text updates to Twitter/X with the x/post-tweet app.
Create media posts through x/post-create when a media URL is available.
Like, retweet, delete, and retrieve posts by tweet ID.
Send direct messages and follow users through inference.sh apps.
Retrieve user profile information by username.
Combine AI image or video generation outputs with Twitter/X posting workflows.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and a logged-in account with Twitter/X integration configured.
Does not provide approval gates or rate-limit controls inside the skill text.
Can create public posts or private messages with real account impact.
Media posting depends on externally hosted media URLs from supported services.

use_cases.title: Publish Campaign Updates
use_cases.description: Draft and post approved campaign updates from a terminal workflow using the configured Twitter/X account.

use_cases.title: Run Engagement Tasks
use_cases.description: Like, retweet, follow, or message specific accounts after reviewing each target and action.

use_cases.title: Post Generated Media
use_cases.description: Generate an image or video with inference.sh apps and publish it to Twitter/X with a matching caption.

prompt_templates.title: Post a Basic Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to prepare a command that posts this text to Twitter/X: [approved text]. Show me the command before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Media Post
prompt_templates.scenario: Post with a hosted image or video
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to create a Twitter/X post with this caption and media URL: [caption], [media URL]. Ask for confirmation before any external action.

prompt_templates.title: Review Engagement Actions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to prepare like, retweet, follow, or direct message commands for these targets: [targets]. List every action for approval first.

prompt_templates.title: Generate and Publish Media
prompt_templates.prompt: Use inference.sh apps to generate media from this prompt, extract the resulting media URL, and prepare a Twitter/X post. Do not publish until I approve the final text and media.

output_examples.input: Post an approved launch note to Twitter/X.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a clear posting command, displays the final tweet text, and waits for explicit approval before execution.

output_examples.input: Send a thank-you direct message to a specific user ID.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the direct message action, confirms the recipient and message text, then runs the approved inference.sh workflow.

output_examples.input: Create an AI image and share it on Twitter/X.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs the media generation workflow, captures the media URL, drafts a caption, and asks for approval before publishing.

best_practices: Require explicit user approval before posting, deleting, following, retweeting, liking, or sending direct messages.
Use test accounts or limited-scope accounts before connecting important brand accounts.
Review generated text, media URLs, target IDs, and recipient IDs before running any command.
anti_patterns: Do not pipe remote installer scripts to a shell without reviewing the source and integrity checks.
Do not run bulk engagement actions without rate limits, approvals, and platform policy review.
Do not place secrets, access tokens, or private customer data in tweet text or command history.

faq.question: Does this skill post directly to Twitter/X?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents inference.sh CLI apps that can create posts when the account is authenticated and authorized.

faq.question: Does it support media posts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can prepare x/post-create workflows that reference a hosted image or video URL.

faq.question: Can it send direct messages?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes an x/dm-send workflow for sending messages to a recipient ID.

faq.question: What setup is required?
faq.answer: You need the inference.sh CLI, an inference.sh login, and a configured Twitter/X integration.

faq.question: Why is this skill marked high risk?
faq.answer: It includes a pipe-to-shell installer and commands that can change a live Twitter/X account.

faq.question: Is confirmed malware present?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The risk comes from supply-chain exposure and real external account actions.
